programmes.show_description: Take Me To Your Leader: Steed and Tara are tasked with tracking the movements of a briefcase that is being passed around between enemy agents. Will the case expose a national security risk?
38 Across: Flummoxed by a crossword puzzle, Hawkeye persuades his old friend, Tippy Brook - an expert at puzzles, to come to the camp.
Ping Pong: The 4077th hosts a traditional Korean wedding, much to Frank's objections. Colonel Potter runs into an old friend who has put his troops in jeopardy to receive a promotion.
End Run: A former football player is distraught by the resulting surgery after a severe leg injury. Frank goes behind Colonel Potter's back to set up a boxing match between Klinger and Zale.
Human Interest: Henry and Matthew find themselves needing to find a new housekeeper when Enid wins money on the premium bonds. Will they be able to agree on a suitable replacement?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Brightest Star: Jonathan and Mark chasten Laurie, a young actress, whose fame is turning her into a monster, fracturing her family and robbing her of her childhood.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
An Investment In Caring: Jonathan must get Helen's neighbours to join together and help save their homes from being seized by corporate developers.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

A Divine Madness: Mr Crook, convinced he is King Arthur, is in danger of being declared incompetent by his son. Can Jonathan work a reconciliation?
Catch A Falling Star: Jonathan and Mark have been hired to look after the kids of a movie star who never has time to spend with his family.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Centennial: Walnut Grove prepares to celebrate the nation's centennial, but the party is threatened with cancellation when their taxes go up.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Soldier's Return: Coming home after 12 years of performing with a busy Philadelphia orchestra, Mrs. Whipple's troubled son returns to Walnut Grove.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Going Home: The Ingalls farm and livelihood lies in tatters when a devastating tornado sweeps through Walnut Grove.
The Grandchild: Part 1: Mary Ellen is worried that bad omens will threaten the health of her unborn child. Part 1 of 2.
The Grandchild: Part 2: Mary Ellen is worried that bad omens will threaten the health of her unborn child. Part 2 of 2.
Eureka: The lure of a goldmine leads Quigley into a race against time. He isn't the only one who believes they've struck gold, as a little help from Orla goes a long way with Conor.
Flying Home: Helen returns to Ormston to join the villagers in paying their last respects to Tom. Meanwhile, Henry suggests that Deborah and Donald sell the cottage hospital to the NHS.
Eleanor and Robert are making slow progress on the underground railway when the train suddenly leaves the rails and crashes into the side of the tunnel.

programmes.show_name: New: Good News on GREAT! Extra
programmes.show_description: An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Old Boy Net: Alongside his pupil Fiona Allways, Rumpole defends a couple charged with blackmail and with running an immoral house. But who are the couple trying to protect?
Hanky Panky: BJ spends the night with a nurse after consoling her about her troubled marriage. Meanwhile Margaret is upset because she hasn't received a letter from her fiancé recently.
Hepatitis: Hawkeye's back pains seem minimal compared to Father Mulcahy, who has arthritis and is jaundiced under the tongue. Hawkeye suspects hepatitis and has to inoculate the staff.
The General's Practitioner: In the midst of Hawkeye being considered for the position of a general's personal physician, Radar becomes a surrogate father to a Korean woman and her baby.
...Likely Lads?: End of an Era: The day of the wedding dawns, and the women are racing around getting ready for the big event. Meanwhile, Terry has his hands full looking after Bob.
...Likely Lads?: Absent Friends: Bob and Thelma are on a Norwegian honeymoon, far away from the influence of Terry - or so they think.
...Likely Lads?: Heart to Heart: Bob comes back from his honeymoon to find that Terry is off his beer and is going out with Thelma's sister Susan. Is Terry in love?
Sleeping Partners: Robin Tripp, now a fully qualified, unemployed chef, harbours ambitions of opening his own bistro...for people who appreciate high prices.
The Bistro Kids: In the run-up to the bistro's launch, Robin must battle a stuffed bear, half a pig and a public health inspector. A tricky first customer causes calamity.
Hanky Panky: BJ spends the night with a nurse after consoling her about her troubled marriage. Meanwhile Margaret is upset because she hasn't received a letter from her fiancé recently.
Hepatitis: Hawkeye's back pains seem minimal compared to Father Mulcahy, who has arthritis and is jaundiced under the tongue. Hawkeye suspects hepatitis and has to inoculate the staff.
The General's Practitioner: In the midst of Hawkeye being considered for the position of a general's personal physician, Radar becomes a surrogate father to a Korean woman and her baby.
Success Story: Matthew is delighted when Henry's successful younger brother Edward comes to stay. But will Henry be proud or envious of his sibling's achievements?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Old Boy Net: Alongside his pupil Fiona Allways, Rumpole defends a couple charged with blackmail and with running an immoral house. But who are the couple trying to protect?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Right Thing: Harry, still grieving over the loss of his wife is placed in a nursing home. His outlook on life changes when he has the opportunity to fulfil his wife's biggest wish.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Thoroughbreds: Part 1: Jonathan and Mark reunite old childhood friends Lizzy MacGill and Garth Armstrong. The two of them elope but encounter some serious problems.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Help Wanted: Angel: An advertisement brings Jonathan and Mark to an elderly scriptwriter who needs an 'angel' to help bankroll his movie about the lonely people in his neighbourhood.
Dust Child: Jonathan and Mark intervene after witnessing the prejudice facing a girl who has begun living with her father's family in the United States.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Collection: After rescuing Reverend Alden from his runaway wagon, down-on-his-luck Caleb Hodgekiss devises a plan to fleece the charitable townsfolk of Walnut Grove.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Bunny: When Nellie Oleson is seriously injured after falling from Bunny, a horse that used to belong to Laura Ingalls, Nellie's mother blames Laura for the accident.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Race: Mrs. Oleson is so convinced that her daughter Nellie's new racehorse will beat Laura's in the Township horse race that she offers-up a family heirloom as first prize.
The First Casualty: The war deeply affects the Waltons when Curt is called into active duty and G.W. Haines is killed during training.
The Battle of Drucilla's Pond: The United States Army invades Walton's Mountain to practise manoeuvres, and Olivia sells all her paintings to a mysterious gentleman.
Behind Bars: Quigley bids farewell to Niamh and discovers the new owner of the pub is an old enemy. Conor's amorous intentions are foiled by Orla.
Community Spirits: Deborah continues to run the hospital with Donald and becomes involved with Janette Locke, who appears to be ill-treating her son.
Eleanor's private practice is failing, and encouraged by Robert, she applies for a part-time job as a dresser at the St Jude's Hospital.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Female of the Species: Rumpole defends Tony Timson, who is accused of being involved in a bungled armed robbery. Meanwhile, Rumpole tries to get Fiona a job at his chambers.
Movie Tonight: Colonel Potter hopes a movie will cure the 4077th's morale problem. But during the show, the staff of the camp are forced to make its own entertainment.
Souvenirs: Hawkeye chastises Willie Stratton, a souvenir junk dealer, for endangering the lives of Korean children. Klinger becomes a pole-sitter in his latest attempt to get discharged.
Post Op: Blood is low and casualties are high as incoming patients arrive at the 4077th. Hawkeye, BJ, Frank, and Hot Lips work in the OR through the night and into the morning with no rest.
Off-site antics with John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Wendy Richard. Grace Bros. is closing for alterations, and the staff are off to Costa Plonka for sun, sea and saucy encounters. Are you free? (1977)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

A Little Competition: Robin knew the restaurant next door would be stiff competition, but it turned out to be more than he bargained for. Vicky meets an old flame.
The Maternal Triangle: Robin is not keen to meet Vicky's mother, as he's heard she has a nasty habit of hitting people on the nose. The new dishwasher thinks he can cope single-handed.
